mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold, or Rank IC remains negative across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and rising consumer prices creates stagflationary pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL both exceed their 6-month moving averages, with a positive Rank IC observed.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening real economic activity that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the regression coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns across all market conditions, with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals collapsing consumer confidence and credit conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods following a 10% housing starts drawdown, invalidating the recession transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate combined with stable unemployment indicates a successful monetary soft landing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in multivariate regression, or forward returns are negative when FEDFUNDS falls while UNRATE remains flat.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product directly increases corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during mid-cycle expansions, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 for the signal.
